Name: gpu_latency_optimizer
Title: Latency-Aware GPU Optimization for Large Language Models via Hybrid CPU-GPU Attention
Short Hypothesis: By leveraging hybrid CPU-GPU attention mechanisms and optimizing memory bandwidth utilization, we can achieve significant latency reductions for large language models on commodity GPUs without compromising accuracy.
Related Work: Recent works such as HGCA and PLAIN have explored hybrid CPU-GPU attention and processing-in-memory architectures for accelerating large language model inference. However, these approaches often focus on specific hardware configurations or require significant modifications to the model architecture. Our proposal builds upon these ideas and aims to develop a more general and flexible framework for latency-aware GPU optimization that can be applied to a wide range of large language models and commodity GPU hardware.
Abstract: We propose a latency-aware GPU optimization framework for large language models that leverages hybrid CPU-GPU attention mechanisms and optimizes memory bandwidth utilization. Our approach combines the benefits of CPU-GPU attention with the efficiency of processing-in-memory architectures, enabling significant latency reductions without compromising accuracy. We evaluate our framework on a range of large language models and commodity GPU hardware, demonstrating its effectiveness in achieving high-throughput and low-latency inference.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Evaluate the effectiveness of hybrid CPU-GPU attention mechanisms in reducing latency for large language models on commodity GPUs.
Experiment 2: Investigate the impact of memory bandwidth utilization on latency and develop optimization strategies to minimize memory access overhead.
Experiment 3: Compare the performance of our framework with state-of-the-art approaches such as HGCA and PLAIN on a range of large language models and commodity GPU hardware.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of integrating hybrid CPU-GPU attention mechanisms with existing large language model architectures.
The potential for increased memory access overhead due to the use of CPU-GPU attention mechanisms.
The need for careful optimization of memory bandwidth utilization to minimize latency.
